title: Update prices
summary: Practice updating prices in the inventory_items table using safe SQL habits. Learn to preview affected rows, use WHERE clauses, and avoid accidental data changes.

sketchBlocks.title: Preview Before Update
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Before updating prices in the `inventory_items` table, always preview the rows you plan to change. For example, to see all items in the 'Accessories' category:

```sql
SELECT id, name, price FROM inventory_items WHERE category = 'Accessories';
```

This helps you confirm which rows will be affected before running an UPDATE.

sketchBlocks.title: Updating Prices Safely
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
To update prices, use the `UPDATE` statement with a `WHERE` clause. For example, to increase the price of all 'Kitchen' items by $1:

```sql
UPDATE inventory_items SET price = price + 1 WHERE category = 'Kitchen';
```

Always include a `WHERE` clause to avoid changing every row by mistake.

quizDraft.title: Update price for a specific item
quizDraft.prompt: Increase the price of the item named 'Sticker Pack' by $2 in the inventory_items table.
quizDraft.hint: Use UPDATE with SET and a WHERE clause filtering by name.

help.concept: To update a specific row, use UPDATE with a WHERE clause to target the correct item.
help.hint_1: Set price = price + 2 where name matches 'Sticker Pack'.
help.hint_2: Make sure to update only the row where name is 'Sticker Pack'.

quizDraft.solutionCode: UPDATE inventory_items SET price = price + 2 WHERE name = 'Sticker Pack';

quizDraft.title: Update prices for a category
quizDraft.prompt: Set the price of all items in the 'Decor' category to 20.00.
quizDraft.hint: Use UPDATE with SET and filter by category.

help.concept: You can update multiple rows by filtering with a WHERE clause on a column value.
help.hint_1: Set price to 20.00 where category is 'Decor'.
help.hint_2: Make sure only 'Decor' items are affected.

quizDraft.solutionCode: UPDATE inventory_items SET price = 20.00 WHERE category = 'Decor';

quizDraft.title: Update prices for test items only
quizDraft.prompt: Reduce the price by $1 for all items marked as test items (is_test = 1).
quizDraft.hint: Use UPDATE with SET and filter using is_test.

help.concept: To update only test items, use a WHERE clause with is_test = 1.
help.hint_1: Subtract 1 from price where is_test is 1.
help.hint_2: Only rows with is_test = 1 should be updated.

quizDraft.solutionCode: UPDATE inventory_items SET price = price - 1 WHERE is_test = 1;

quizDraft.title: Safe update practices
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are good practices when updating prices in a table? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Think about how to avoid accidental changes.

help.concept: Previewing and filtering updates helps prevent mistakes. Avoid updating all rows unless intentional.
help.hint_1: Preview with SELECT and use WHERE to target updates.
help.hint_2: Avoid running UPDATE without WHERE unless you want to change every row.

options: a. Preview affected rows with SELECT before running UPDATE
b. Always use a WHERE clause to target specific rows
c. Run UPDATE without WHERE to save time
d. Double-check your filter conditions before executing UPDATE

quizDraft.title: Fill in the blank: WHERE clause importance
quizDraft.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence: You should almost always use a ______ clause when running an UPDATE statement.
quizDraft.hint: This clause limits which rows are affected.

help.concept: The WHERE clause specifies which rows to update, preventing accidental changes to all rows.
help.hint_1: It's the clause that filters rows in UPDATE and SELECT.
help.hint_2: Without this clause, every row in the table will be updated.

quizDraft.template: You should almost always use a ______ clause when running an UPDATE statement.
